column3: Question
column5: Solution / Hints

column3: Check whether a string is a palindrome after ignoring case and punctuation.
column5: Two pointers from both ends. Skip non-alphanumeric. Compare lowercased. O(n).

column3: Solve two-sum in a sorted array.
column5: Left and right pointers. Move left if sum < target, move right if sum > target. O(n).

column3: Find all triplets with sum zero or equal to a target.
column5: Sort. Fix one element, two-pointer on rest. Skip duplicates. O(n²).

column3: Find all quadruplets with sum equal to a target.
column5: Sort. Fix two elements, two-pointer on rest. Skip duplicates. O(n³).

column3: Find the container with the most water.
column5: Two pointers from both ends. Move the shorter line inward. Area = min(h) × width. O(n).

column3: Compute trapped rain water.
column5: Two pointers with leftMax and rightMax. Move from the side with smaller max. O(n) time O(1) space.

column3: Remove duplicates from a sorted array in place.
column5: Slow (write) and fast (read) pointers. Write when new element differs. O(n).

column3: Move all zeroes to the end while preserving relative order.
column5: Two pointers: one for next non-zero position. Swap non-zero elements forward. O(n).

column3: Merge two sorted arrays in place.
column5: Start from end of both arrays. Place larger element at back. O(m+n).

column3: Return the sorted squares of a sorted array.
column5: Two pointers from both ends. Compare absolute values, fill result array from back. O(n).

column3: Decide whether removing at most one character can make a string a palindrome.
column5: Two pointers. On mismatch, try skipping left or right char and check remaining substring. O(n).

column3: Find common elements across three sorted arrays in linear time.
column5: Three pointers, one per array. Advance the pointer with the smallest value. O(n1+n2+n3).

column3: Merge three sorted arrays into one sorted array with duplicates removed.
column5: Three-pointer merge. Skip duplicates during insertion. O(n1+n2+n3).

column3: Detect a loop in a linked list.
column5: Slow (1 step) and fast (2 steps). If they meet, cycle exists. O(n) time O(1) space.

column3: Find the starting node of the loop in a linked list.
column5: After detection, reset one pointer to head. Both move 1 step. Meeting point = cycle start.

column3: Find the middle node of a linked list.
column5: Slow and fast pointers. When fast reaches end, slow is at middle. O(n).

column3: Remove the nth node from the end.
column5: Two pointers with n gap. When fast reaches end, slow is at (n+1)th from end. O(n) one pass.

column3: Check whether a linked list is a palindrome.
column5: Find mid (slow/fast), reverse second half, compare with first half, restore. O(n) time O(1) space.

column3: Find the repeated number (Floyd's on array).
column5: Treat values as next pointers. Floyd's cycle detection on array. O(n) time O(1) space.

column3: Find the length of the longest substring without repeating characters.
column5: Expand right, shrink left on duplicate using hashset/map. Track maxLen. O(n).

column3: Find the longest substring with at most k distinct characters.
column5: Expand right, shrink left when distinct > k using hashmap of char counts. O(n).

column3: Find the minimum window substring containing all characters of another string.
column5: Two hashmaps. Expand right until valid. Shrink left while still valid. Track min. O(n+m).

column3: Find the maximum consecutive 1s if you may flip at most k zeroes.
column5: Track zero count in window. Shrink left when zeros > k. Track max window size. O(n).

column3: Find the minimum-length subarray with sum at least a target.
column5: Expand right. Shrink left while sum >= target. Track minLen. O(n) for positive nums.

column3: Count the number of subarrays with product less than k.
column5: Expand right, shrink left when product >= k. Count += (right − left + 1) each step. O(n).

column3: Solve Fruit Into Baskets or longest subarray with at most two distinct values.
column5: Sliding window + hashmap. Shrink left when distinct > 2. Same pattern as Q21. O(n).

column3: Find the longest repeating-character window after changing at most k characters.
column5: Track maxFreq in window. Shrink when windowLen − maxFreq > k. O(n).

column3: Find the minimum swaps needed to group all elements <= k together.
column5: Count elements <= k = window size. Slide fixed window, count bad elements. Min bad = answer.

column3: Find the maximum sum or average of a fixed-size window.
column5: Add right element, subtract left element. Track max. O(n).

column3: Find the sliding window maximum using a deque or monotonic queue.
column5: Monotonic decreasing deque. Remove smaller from back, expired from front. O(n).

column3: Design a moving average over a data stream.
column5: Queue of fixed size. Maintain running sum. Add new, remove old when full. O(1) per add.

column3: Design a hit counter over a moving time window.
column5: Queue of timestamps. Dequeue expired on query. Or circular buffer of size 300. O(1) amortized.

column3: Count the number of subarrays whose sum is exactly k.
column5: prefixSum hashmap. For each sum, check if (sum − k) exists in map. O(n).

column3: Count the number of subarrays whose sum is divisible by k.
column5: prefixSum mod k + hashmap. Same remainder → subarray divisible by k. Handle negative mods.

column3: Count the number of subarrays with equal numbers of 0s and 1s.
column5: Replace 0 with −1. Problem becomes 'subarrays with sum 0'. Prefix sum + hashmap. O(n).

column3: Find the length of the longest zero-sum subarray.
column5: Prefix sum + hashmap storing first occurrence of each sum. Max length = i − first[sum]. O(n).

column3: Find an equilibrium or pivot index.
column5: Compute total. Scan left-to-right: if leftSum == total − leftSum − arr[i], found. O(n).

column3: Compute the product of array except self.
column5: Two-pass: prefix products left→right, suffix products right→left. O(n) without division.

column3: Find the intersection of two arrays including handling duplicates.
column5: HashMap to count elements of one array. Iterate second and decrement. O(m+n).

column3: Find the longest consecutive sequence in an unsorted array.
column5: HashSet. For each num where num−1 not in set, count forward. O(n) amortized.

column3: Count distinct pairs with difference equal to k.
column5: HashSet: for each element check if element+k exists. Or sort + two pointers. O(n).

column3: Find the first non-repeating character in a string.
column5: Frequency map pass 1. Pass 2: first char with count 1. O(n).

column3: Determine whether two strings are anagrams.
column5: Frequency count array of size 26. Increment for s1, decrement for s2. Check all zero. O(n).

column3: Group a list of strings into anagram buckets.
column5: HashMap with sorted string (or char-frequency tuple) as key. O(n·k log k).

column3: Count how many distinct strings are possible after exactly one swap.
column5: Try all O(n²) swaps with a set. Optimize with character frequency analysis.

column3: Find the top-k frequent elements.
column5: HashMap for freq + min-heap of size k. Or bucket sort by frequency. O(n log k) or O(n).

column3: Sort characters by frequency using a heap.
column5: Frequency map + max-heap (or bucket sort). Build string from heap. O(n log k).

column3: Find the maximum subarray sum.
column5: currentMax = max(num, currentMax + num). Track globalMax. O(n) time O(1) space.

column3: Find the maximum product subarray.
column5: Track both maxProd and minProd (negatives flip). Reset on zero. O(n).

column3: Find the maximum subarray sum with one deletion.
column5: Two arrays: maxEndHere[i] (no deletion) and maxEndHereWithDel[i] (one deletion). O(n).

column3: Find the majority element that appears more than n/2 times.
column5: Maintain candidate and count. Increment on match, decrement on mismatch. Last candidate wins. O(n) O(1).

column3: Find all elements that appear more than n/3 times.
column5: Extended with two candidates. At most 2 such elements. Verify counts in second pass. O(n).

column3: Find the first missing positive integer.
column5: Place each num at index num−1. First index where arr[i] ≠ i+1 is answer. O(n) time O(1) space.

column3: Find the repeated number and the missing number.
column5: Cyclic sort: place each val at index val−1. The misplaced position reveals both numbers.

column3: Rearrange a stream of three kinds of objects so equal objects are grouped together.
column5: Three pointers: low, mid, high. Swap mid with low or high based on value. O(n) single pass.

column3: Find an integer square root or a square root to fixed precision.
column5: Binary search on [0, n]. Check mid×mid ≤ n. For precision, float binary search. O(log n).

column3: Search in a row-wise and column-wise sorted matrix.
column5: Start top-right. Go left if target < current, down if target > current. O(m+n).

column3: Find the first and last occurrence of a target in a sorted array.
column5: Two binary searches: leftmost (continue left on equal) and rightmost (continue right on equal). O(log n).

column3: Search in a rotated sorted array.
column5: Determine which half is sorted. Check if target lies in sorted half. O(log n).

column3: Find the minimum element in a rotated sorted array.
column5: Compare mid with right. If mid > right, min is in right half. O(log n).

column3: Find a peak element.
column5: If mid < mid+1, peak is right; else left. O(log n).

column3: Find the k closest elements in a sorted array.
column5: Binary search for insertion point. Expand outward with two pointers. O(log n + k).

column3: Search a value in an infinite or unknown-size sorted array.
column5: Exponential search: double index until exceeding target, then binary search in range. O(log n).

column3: Find the median of two sorted arrays.
column5: Binary search on smaller array. Partition both. Check boundary conditions. O(log(min(m,n))).

column3: Use binary search on answer to minimize the maximum pages or tasks assigned.
column5: Search on max pages. Feasibility: greedy left→right allocation. O(n log sum).

column3: Find the kth smallest element in a sorted matrix.
column5: Binary search on value range [min, max]. Count elements ≤ mid per row. O(n log(max−min)).

column3: Maximize the minimum distance between placed items using binary search on answer.
column5: Search on distance. Greedy check: place items left→right with min gap. O(n log range).

column3: Find the minimum feasible ship capacity to deliver packages within d days.
column5: Search on capacity [max(w), sum(w)]. Greedy simulate loading. O(n log sum).

column3: Split an array to minimize the largest segment sum.
column5: Search on answer. Check if ≤ m parts with each ≤ mid. O(n log sum).

column3: Find the minimum feasible time to finish trips/jobs/machines using a monotonic predicate.
column5: Search on time. Check if total work at time t ≥ required. O(n log maxTime).

column3: Count inversions in an array.
column5: Modified merge sort. Count inversions during merge step when left[i] > right[j]. O(n log n).

column3: Sort a linked list using merge sort.
column5: Find mid (slow/fast), split, recursively sort halves, merge. O(n log n).

column3: Find the second largest element in one pass without extra space.
column5: Track two variables: largest and secondLargest. Single pass O(n) O(1) space.

column3: Find the next greater number using the same digits.
column5: Find rightmost ascending pair. Swap with smallest larger digit to its right. Reverse suffix. O(n).

column3: Find the next greater element for every array item.
column5: Monotonic decreasing stack. Traverse right→left (or left→right). Pop smaller. O(n).

column3: Find the next smaller element for every array item.
column5: Monotonic increasing stack. Similar but track smaller elements. O(n).

column3: Find the largest rectangle in a histogram.
column5: Monotonic increasing stack of indices. On pop, compute area with popped bar as shortest. O(n).

column3: Find the maximal rectangle in a binary matrix.
column5: Build histogram row by row. Apply largest-rectangle-in-histogram per row. O(m×n).

column3: Solve Daily Temperatures.
column5: Monotonic decreasing stack of indices. On warmer day, pop and record distance. O(n).

column3: Solve Stock Span.
column5: Monotonic decreasing stack of (price, index). Span = current index − stack top index. O(n).

column3: Find the next greater element in a circular array.
column5: Traverse array twice (2n). Monotonic stack with index % n. O(n).

column3: Remove k digits to form the smallest possible number.
column5: Monotonic increasing stack. Remove larger digit from top when smaller comes. Greedy. O(n).

column3: Count square submatrices with all ones.
column5: DP: dp[i][j] = min(left, up, diagonal) + 1. Sum all dp values. O(m×n).

column3: Evaluate a postfix expression.
column5: Push operands. On operator, pop two, compute, push result. O(n).

column3: Convert an infix expression to postfix.
column5: Shunting-yard algorithm. Stack for operators. Respect precedence and associativity. O(n).

column3: Evaluate an arithmetic expression with operators and parentheses.
column5: Two stacks (values, operators) or recursive descent. Handle precedence. O(n).

column3: Check for balanced parentheses or brackets.
column5: Stack. Push open brackets. Pop on close and check match. Empty stack at end = balanced. O(n).

column3: Simplify a Unix-style path.
column5: Split by '/'. Push directory names. Pop on '..'. Ignore '.' and empty. Join with '/'.

column3: Resolve collision-style stack simulations such as Asteroid Collision.
column5: Stack. Positive goes on. Negative collides with positive tops. Resolve by size. O(n).

column3: Decode bracket-encoded strings such as 3[a2[c]].
column5: Stack: push count and current string on '['. On ']', pop, repeat, concatenate. O(output length).

column3: Implement a queue using two stacks.
column5: Push to s1. Pop: if s2 empty, transfer all from s1. Pop from s2. Amortized O(1).

column3: Implement a stack using queues.
column5: Push: enqueue, then rotate n−1 times. Pop: dequeue. O(n) push or O(n) pop.

column3: Design a min stack.
column5: Two stacks: main + min. Push to min when ≤ current min. O(1) getMin.

column3: Print the first non-repeating character at every step of a character stream.
column5: Queue + frequency map. Dequeue while front has freq > 1. O(n).

column3: Reverse a singly linked list.
column5: Iterative: three pointers (prev, curr, next). Or recursive. O(n) time O(1) space iterative.

column3: Reverse a linked list in groups of size k.
column5: Reverse first k nodes. Recursively reverse remaining. Connect groups. O(n).

column3: Merge two sorted linked lists.
column5: Compare heads, attach smaller, advance pointer. Iterative or recursive. O(m+n).

column3: Clone a linked list with random pointer.
column5: Interleave cloned nodes → set random pointers → separate lists. O(n) without hashmap.

column3: Insert into a sorted circular linked list when only an arbitrary node is given.
column5: Traverse to find correct position. Handle wrap-around case (max→min). O(n).

column3: Find the intersection point of two linked lists.
column5: Get lengths, advance longer by diff, walk together. Or two-pointer cycle trick. O(m+n).

column3: Add two numbers represented by linked lists.
column5: Traverse both, add digits + carry. New nodes per digit. Handle unequal lengths & final carry. O(max(m,n)).

column3: Flatten a multilevel linked list.
column5: DFS / stack. On child, splice child list between current and next. O(n).

column3: Reorder a linked list by odd and even positions or partition it around a pivot.
column5: Separate into two lists (odd/even indexed), then concatenate. O(n).

column3: Select a random node from a singly linked list.
column5: Reservoir sampling. For ith node, pick with probability 1/i. O(n) per call O(1) space.

column3: Find the maximum profit from one stock transaction.
column5: Track minPrice so far. profit = price − minPrice. Track maxProfit. O(n).

column3: Find the maximum profit from multiple stock transactions.
column5: Add all positive diffs: prices[i] − prices[i−1] when positive. O(n).

column3: Solve Jump Game.
column5: Track farthest reachable index. If i > farthest, return false. O(n).

column3: Solve Jump Game II or minimum jumps to reach the end.
column5: Track currentEnd and farthest. Increment jumps when reaching currentEnd. BFS-like. O(n).

column3: Find the minimum number of taps needed to water a garden.
column5: Convert to intervals. Greedy interval covering: always pick the tap reaching farthest. O(n).

column3: Solve the gas station complete-circuit problem.
column5: Track totalSurplus and currentSurplus. Reset start on negative current. If total ≥ 0, answer = start. O(n).

column3: Solve the lemonade change greedy simulation.
column5: Track $5 and $10 bills. For $10: give $5. For $20: prefer $10+$5, else 3×$5. O(n).

column3: Distribute candy subject to neighbor constraints.
column5: Two passes: left→right (compare left neighbor), right→left (compare right). Take max. O(n).

column3: Assign cookies or resources greedily to maximize satisfied demand.
column5: Sort both arrays. Two pointers: assign smallest sufficient cookie to smallest child. O(n log n).

column3: Solve reverse-greedy transformations such as Broken Calculator.
column5: Work backwards. If target > start: even → divide; odd → add 1. Greedy reverse. O(log target).

column3: Solve job sequencing with deadlines and profits.
column5: Sort by profit desc. For each job, find latest free slot ≤ deadline using DSU or array. O(n log n).

column3: Reconstruct a queue by height and position constraints.
column5: Sort by height desc, k asc. Insert each person at index k in result. O(n²).

column3: Reorganize a string so no two adjacent characters are the same.
column5: Max-heap by frequency. Greedily place most frequent, then next. Return '' if impossible. O(n log k).

column3: Find the lexicographically smallest string obtainable by removing at most one character.
column5: Find first i where s[i] > s[i+1], remove s[i]. If none found, remove last char. O(n).

column3: Partition a string or label sequence greedily so each symbol appears in at most one part.
column5: Record last occurrence of each char. Expand partition end to max last occurrence. Cut when i == end. O(n).

column3: Merge overlapping intervals.
column5: Sort by start. Merge if current start ≤ prev end. Update end = max(ends). O(n log n).

column3: Insert an interval into a sorted non-overlapping list.
column5: Add non-overlapping before. Merge overlapping ones. Add non-overlapping after. O(n).

column3: Find interval intersections between two lists.
column5: Two pointers. If overlap, add intersection. Advance the one ending first. O(m+n).

column3: Check whether all meetings can be attended; if not find rooms needed.
column5: Sort by start for attendance check. Min-heap of end times for room count. O(n log n).

column3: Perform activity selection or maximize non-overlapping intervals.
column5: Sort by end time. Greedily pick earliest-ending non-overlapping. O(n log n).

column3: Find the minimum number of intervals to remove so the rest do not overlap.
column5: Sort by end. Count non-overlapping (activity selection). Remove = total − count. O(n log n).

column3: Find the minimum number of arrows to burst all balloons.
column5: Sort by end. Shoot at first end. Skip covered balloons. Count uncovered. O(n log n).

column3: Find the point where the maximum number of intervals overlap.
column5: Events: +1 at start, −1 at end. Sort. Track running sum and max. O(n log n).

column3: Find the minimum number of platforms or rooms needed for intervals.
column5: Sort arrivals and departures. Two-pointer merge. Or sweep line. O(n log n).

column3: Find the peak number of concurrent intervals.
column5: Same sweep line pattern. Max running sum = peak concurrency. O(n log n).

column3: Solve car-pooling or capacity scheduling with a sweep-line technique.
column5: +passengers at pickup, −passengers at drop. Sort events. Check if capacity ever exceeded. O(n log n).

column3: Find the number of rooms needed for meetings using a min-heap.
column5: Sort by start. Min-heap of end times. If earliest end ≤ new start, reuse. Else add room. O(n log n).

column3: Find the shortest path in an unweighted graph using BFS.
column5: Standard BFS from source. Distance = level in BFS tree. O(V+E).

column3: Find the shortest path in a binary maze.
column5: BFS on grid. 4-directional. Walls = 0, paths = 1. O(m×n).

column3: Find the shortest path in a binary matrix.
column5: BFS from (0,0) to (n−1,n−1). 8-directional movement. Track visited. O(m×n).

column3: Find the minimum knight moves and print one valid path.
column5: BFS with 8 L-shaped moves. Track parent for path reconstruction. O(area).

column3: Solve Word Ladder.
column5: BFS. Each word = node. Edge = differ by 1 char. Use wildcard patterns for efficiency. O(n×L²).

column3: Solve Open the Lock.
column5: BFS from '0000'. 8 neighbors per state (4 wheels × 2 directions). Skip deadends. O(10⁴).

column3: Solve Snakes and Ladders with BFS.
column5: BFS from cell 1. Try 1–6 dice rolls. Follow snakes/ladders instantly. O(n²).

column3: Determine whether all rooms can be visited in Keys and Rooms.
column5: BFS/DFS from room 0. Collect keys, visit new rooms. Check if all visited. O(V+E).

column3: Count the number of islands.
column5: For each unvisited '1', BFS/DFS flood fill and increment count. O(m×n).

column3: Count the number of distinct islands.
column5: DFS with shape encoding (relative positions or direction sequence). HashSet of shapes. O(m×n).

column3: Find the maximum area of an island.
column5: BFS/DFS flood fill. Track area of each island. Return maximum. O(m×n).

column3: Solve rotten oranges with multi-source BFS.
column5: Enqueue all rotten initially. BFS layer by layer. Time = layers. Check fresh remaining. O(m×n).

column3: Perform flood fill.
column5: DFS/BFS from starting cell. Change color of connected same-color cells. O(m×n).

column3: Count the number of connected components in a graph.
column5: BFS/DFS from each unvisited node. Or DSU. Count components. O(V+E).

column3: Solve the water jug problem both as state-space search and with gcd reasoning.
column5: BFS on states (a,b). Or math: solvable iff target % gcd(jug1,jug2) == 0. O(jug1×jug2) for BFS.

column3: Find a path with minimum effort in a grid.
column5: Dijkstra: state = (row, col). Weight = max abs diff along path. O(m×n log(m×n)).

column3: Solve Swim in Rising Water.
column5: Dijkstra with max edge weight. Or binary search on time + BFS. O(n² log n).

column3: Find the shortest path with alternating edge colors.
column5: BFS with state = (node, last_color). Expand with alternating color. O(V+E).

column3: Find the cheapest flight within k stops.
column5: Modified Bellman-Ford (k+1 iterations). Or BFS with level limit. O(k×E).

column3: Find the shortest path in a weighted graph using Dijkstra's algorithm.
column5: Min-heap priority queue. Relax edges greedily. No negative weights. O((V+E) log V).

column3: Solve Network Delay Time.
column5: Dijkstra from source. Answer = max distance to any node. −1 if unreachable. O((V+E) log V).

column3: Find the minimum cost to connect all points.
column5: MST: Prim's (min-heap) or Kruskal's (sort edges + DSU). Manhattan distance. O(n² log n).

column3: Evaluate division queries on a graph.
column5: Build weighted directed graph. DFS/BFS to find path and multiply edge weights. O(Q×(V+E)).

column3: Solve course schedule by detecting a cycle in a directed graph.
column5: Kahn's BFS (in-degree) or DFS 3-coloring. Cycle if not all nodes processed. O(V+E).

column3: Perform topological sort.
column5: Kahn's: BFS with in-degree tracking. Or DFS: add to stack on finish, reverse. O(V+E).

column3: Find eventual safe states in a directed graph.
column5: Reverse graph + topo sort. Or DFS 3-coloring. Safe = nodes not in any cycle. O(V+E).

column3: Infer an alien dictionary order from sorted words.
column5: Build DAG from adjacent word comparisons. Topological sort. Detect contradictions. O(C).

column3: Enumerate all paths from source to target in a DAG.
column5: DFS/backtracking. No cycles → explore all paths. Record when target reached. O(2ⁿ × n).

column3: Implement disjoint set union with path compression and union by rank.
column5: find(x): path compression. union(x,y): attach smaller rank under larger. Near O(1) amortized.

column3: Use DSU for component counting merges and connectivity queries.
column5: Initialize each as own parent. Union on edges. Components = distinct roots.

column3: Detect a cycle in an undirected graph.
column5: Process edges. If both endpoints in same set → cycle. O(E × α(V)).

column3: Find a minimum spanning tree.
column5: Kruskal's: sort edges + DSU. Accept edge if endpoints in different sets. O(E log E).

column3: Process dynamic island additions or Number of Islands II with DSU.
column5: For each new land, union with adjacent land. Track component count. O(k × α(n)).

column3: Merge accounts using a graph or DSU.
column5: Union accounts sharing same email. Group by root parent. O(n × k × α(n)).

column3: Find a redundant connection.
column5: Process edges with DSU. First edge connecting already-connected nodes is redundant. O(n × α(n)).

column3: Detect a cycle in a directed graph.
column5: DFS with 3-color (white/gray/black). Back edge (gray→gray) = cycle. O(V+E).

column3: Check whether a graph is bipartite.
column5: BFS/DFS 2-coloring. If adjacent nodes get same color, not bipartite. O(V+E).

column3: Clone a graph.
column5: DFS/BFS with hashmap (original→clone). Clone neighbors recursively. O(V+E).

column3: Find critical connections or bridges in a network.
column5: DFS with disc[] and low[]. Bridge if low[v] > disc[u] for edge u→v. O(V+E).

column3: Find articulation points in a graph.
column5: Root is AP if ≥ 2 DFS children. Non-root u is AP if child v has low[v] ≥ disc[u]. O(V+E).

column3: Find the maximum depth of a binary tree.
column5: 1 + max(leftDepth, rightDepth). Base: null → 0. O(n).

column3: Find the minimum depth of a binary tree.
column5: BFS returns first leaf level. Or recursive: handle single-child nodes carefully. O(n).

column3: Check whether a binary tree is height-balanced.
column5: Return −1 if unbalanced. Check |leftH − rightH| ≤ 1 at each node. O(n).

column3: Compute the diameter of a binary tree.
column5: Diameter through node = leftHeight + rightHeight. Track global max. O(n).

column3: Compute the maximum path sum in a binary tree.
column5: At each node: single path = val + max(left, right, 0). Global = val + left + right. O(n).

column3: Find the largest subtree that is also a BST.
column5: Return (isBST, size, min, max) per subtree. Track global max size. O(n).

column3: Count univalue subtrees.
column5: Subtree is univalue if children are univalue and same value as root. O(n).

column3: Find the maximum sum of tree nodes such that no two adjacent nodes are chosen.
column5: DP: include (val + grandchildren) vs exclude (children sums). Return pair. O(n).

column3: Find leaves of a binary tree in rounds.
column5: Leaf height = 0. Internal = 1 + max(children). Group by height. O(n).

column3: Check whether one tree is a subtree of another.
column5: For each node in main tree, check subtree match. O(m×n). Or serialize + KMP.

column3: Convert a BST into a greater-sum tree.
column5: Reverse inorder (right→root→left). Maintain running sum. Replace each value. O(n).

column3: Solve root-to-leaf path sum existence.
column5: Subtract node value from target. At leaf, check remaining == 0. O(n).

column3: Print all root-to-leaf paths whose sum equals a target.
column5: DFS with path tracking and backtracking. Record path when leaf + sum matches. O(n).

column3: Count good nodes in a binary tree.
column5: DFS passing max-so-far. Node is good if val ≥ max-so-far. O(n).

column3: Turn a binary tree upside down under a specified parent-child transform.
column5: Leftmost leaf → new root. Parent → right child, sibling → left. Iterative or recursive.

column3: Perform level-order traversal.
column5: BFS with queue. Process level by level using queue size. O(n).

column3: Perform zigzag level-order traversal.
column5: BFS. Alternate direction per level using a flag or deque. O(n).

column3: Perform vertical-order traversal.
column5: BFS/DFS with (column, row). TreeMap<col, list>. Sort by col then row. O(n log n).

column3: Print the top view or bottom view of a binary tree.
column5: BFS with horizontal distance. Top: first node per HD. Bottom: last node per HD. O(n).

column3: Perform diagonal traversal.
column5: Group nodes by diagonal d. Left child: d+1, right child: same d. O(n).

column3: Print the left view or right view of a binary tree.
column5: BFS: first (left) or last (right) node of each level. Or DFS with level tracking. O(n).

column3: Perform boundary traversal.
column5: Left boundary (top-down) + leaves (left→right) + right boundary (bottom-up). No duplicates. O(n).

column3: Compute the sum of leaf nodes at the minimum level.
column5: BFS. At first level with a leaf, sum all leaves at that level. O(n).

column3: Find the maximum width of a binary tree.
column5: BFS with position indexing. Width = rightmost − leftmost + 1. Use offset for overflow. O(n).

column3: Find all nodes at distance k from a target node.
column5: Build parent pointers (BFS/DFS). Then BFS k levels from target. O(n).

column3: Find the amount of time needed to infect an entire binary tree from a start node.
column5: Build graph (parent pointers). BFS from start. Time = max BFS depth. O(n).

column3: Reverse the odd levels of a binary tree.
column5: BFS level by level. At odd levels, reverse the node values. O(n).

column3: Compute range sum in a BST.
column5: If node < low → go right. If node > high → go left. Else add value and go both. O(n).

column3: Find two nodes in a BST whose values sum to k.
column5: Inorder → sorted array → two pointers. Or BST iterator + reverse iterator. O(n).

column3: Find the inorder successor in a BST.
column5: If right child: go right then leftmost. Else: ancestor where node is in left subtree. O(h).

column3: Find the predecessor and successor of a key in a BST.
column5: Traverse. Update predecessor going right, successor going left. O(h).

column3: Find the closest value or k closest values in a BST.
column5: BST search for closest. For k closest: inorder + sliding window, or pred/succ stacks. O(h + k).

column3: Build or reason about a tree from inorder and level-order traversal.
column5: Root from level-order first element. Partition inorder. Recursively filter level-order.

column3: Serialize and deserialize a binary tree.
column5: Preorder with null markers. Serialize: node,left,right. Deserialize: read preorder, build recursively.

column3: Find the lowest common ancestor in a binary tree.
column5: If node is p or q, return it. Recurse left and right. If both non-null, current is LCA. O(n).

column3: Find the lowest common ancestor in an N-ary tree.
column5: Count target nodes found in children subtrees. If ≥ 2, current is LCA. O(n).

column3: Find the k largest distinct elements without fully sorting the array.
column5: Min-heap of size k. Iterate array. O(n log k). Or Quickselect O(n) average.

column3: Find the kth smallest or kth largest element in an array.
column5: Quickselect O(n) avg. Or min/max heap of size k: O(n log k).

column3: Merge k sorted linked lists.
column5: Min-heap of size k with heads. Extract min, push next from that list. O(n log k).

column3: Merge k sorted arrays and optionally remove duplicates.
column5: Min-heap of (value, array_idx, elem_idx). Extract min, push next. Skip dups. O(n log k).

column3: Maintain the median of a data stream.
column5: Two heaps: maxHeap (lower half), minHeap (upper half). Balance sizes. Median from tops. O(log n).

column3: Implement heap sort and explain heapify.
column5: Build max-heap O(n). Repeatedly extract max and sift-down. O(n log n). Heapify = sift down.

column3: Connect ropes or merge files with minimum total cost.
column5: Min-heap. Always merge two smallest. Total = sum of intermediate costs. O(n log n).

column3: Find the smallest range covering at least one element from each of k lists.
column5: Min-heap with one per list. Track max. Range = [heap_min, max]. Advance min's list. O(n log k).

column3: Maximize capital or profit under project-selection constraints such as IPO.
column5: Sort by capital. Min-heap for capital, max-heap for affordable profits. Greedy. O(n log n).

column3: Find the k closest points to the origin.
column5: Max-heap of size k by distance. Or Quickselect. O(n log k) or O(n).

column3: Sort a nearly sorted array where each element is at most k positions away.
column5: Min-heap of size k+1. Extract min for each element. O(n log k).

column3: Maintain the kth largest element in a stream.
column5: Min-heap of size k. If new > top, replace and heapify. Top = kth largest. O(log k) per add.

column3: Schedule tasks with cooldown constraints using a heap-based strategy.
column5: Max-heap by freq. Each cycle: pick top n+1 tasks. Idle if insufficient. O(tasks × log 26).

column3: Design an iterator over k sorted lists or streams.
column5: Min-heap of (value, list_idx, elem_idx). Extract min, push next. O(log k) per next().

column3: Solve Rat in a Maze.
column5: Try all 4 directions. Mark visited. Backtrack on dead end. O(4^(n²)).

column3: Solve N-Queens.
column5: Place row by row. Check column, diagonal, anti-diagonal. O(n!).

column3: Solve Sudoku.
column5: Try 1–9 in each empty cell. Validate row, column, 3×3 box. O(9^(empty cells)).

column3: Generate all permutations of an array or string.
column5: Swap or visited-array approach. n! permutations. O(n × n!).

column3: Generate the power set or all subsets.
column5: Include/exclude each element. Or iterative bitmask. 2ⁿ subsets.

column3: Solve Combination Sum and its common variants.
column5: Sort for pruning. Allow reuse (I) or not (II). Handle dups (II). O(2^target).

column3: Partition a string into palindromic pieces.
column5: Try each prefix. If palindrome, recurse on suffix. O(n × 2ⁿ).

column3: Generate balanced parentheses.
column5: Track open/close count. '(' if open < n, ')' if close < open. O(4ⁿ/√n).

column3: Solve Word Search in a grid.
column5: DFS from each matching cell. Mark visited. Try 4 directions. O(m×n×4^L).

column3: Generate all palindrome permutations of a string.
column5: Check feasibility (≤1 odd-freq char). Generate half-permutations, mirror.

column3: Generate all letter combinations of a phone number.
column5: Map digits to letters. Recurse generating all combos. O(4ⁿ).

column3: Restore all valid IP addresses from a digit string.
column5: Try 1–3 digit segments (0–255). Exactly 4 segments. No leading zeros. Bounded O(1).

column3: Partition an array into k equal-sum subsets.
column5: Target = sum/k. Assign each element to a bucket. Sort desc for pruning. O(k^n).

column3: Insert operators into a digit string to reach a target value.
column5: Try +, −, × between digits. Track cumulative and last operand for ×. O(4ⁿ).

column3: Solve graph coloring or M-coloring.
column5: Try each of M colors per vertex. Check adjacency constraints. O(M^V).

column3: Form a square from matchsticks or solve equivalent subset-assignment.
column5: Partition into 4 equal subsets. Backtracking with pruning. Sort desc. O(4ⁿ).

column3: Generate unique permutations when duplicates are present.
column5: Sort. Skip if same as prev and prev not used in current branch. O(n × n!).

column3: Count ways to reach the nth stair and common variants.
column5: dp[i] = dp[i−1] + dp[i−2]. Fibonacci-like. O(1) space possible. O(n).

column3: Find the minimum cost to climb stairs.
column5: dp[i] = cost[i] + min(dp[i−1], dp[i−2]). O(n).

column3: Find the maximum sum of non-adjacent elements.
column5: dp[i] = max(dp[i−1], dp[i−2] + arr[i]). O(n) time O(1) space.

column3: Solve House Robber.
column5: Same pattern: dp[i] = max(skip, rob). O(n).

column3: Solve House Robber II.
column5: Circular: run House Robber on [0..n−2] and [1..n−1]. Take max. O(n).

column3: Count the number of ways to decode a digit string to letters.
column5: dp[i] = dp[i−1] (valid single) + dp[i−2] (valid pair 10–26). O(n).

column3: Solve Word Break.
column5: dp[i] = true if any dp[j] and s[j..i] in dict. O(n² × L) or trie-based.

column3: Find the longest increasing subsequence.
column5: O(n²) DP. Or O(n log n) patience sorting with binary search on tails array.

column3: Find the longest arithmetic subsequence.
column5: dp[i][diff] = length of AP ending at i with diff. HashMap per index. O(n²).

column3: Find the longest run of consecutive 1s in a binary array.
column5: Linear scan. Reset counter on 0, update max on 1. O(n).

column3: Find the nth ugly number or its variants.
column5: Three pointers for multiples of 2, 3, 5. dp[i] = min(dp[p2]×2, dp[p3]×3, dp[p5]×5). O(n).

column3: Print the maximum number of As using four keys.
column5: dp[i] = i for i ≤ 6. dp[i] = max(dp[j] × (i−j−1)) for breakpoints j. O(n²).

column3: Solve longest common subsequence.
column5: dp[i][j] = dp[i−1][j−1]+1 if match, else max(dp[i−1][j], dp[i][j−1]). O(m×n).

column3: Solve edit distance.
column5: dp[i][j] = dp[i−1][j−1] if match. Else 1 + min(insert, delete, replace). O(m×n).

column3: Count distinct subsequences.
Solve wildcard matching.
column5: '*' matches empty or any prefix. dp[i][j] = dp[i−1][j] || dp[i][j−1] for '*'. O(m×n).

column3: Solve regex-style matching with . and *.
column5: '.' matches any. 'x*' matches 0+ x. Careful * handling in 2D DP. O(m×n).

column3: Solve interleaving string.
column5: dp[i][j] = true if s3[i+j−1] matches s1[i] (with dp[i−1][j]) or s2[j] (with dp[i][j−1]). O(m×n).

column3: Solve Word Break II.
column5: Backtracking + memoization. Return all valid sentences. Cache per start index.

column3: Solve 0/1 knapsack.
column5: dp[i][w] = max(skip, take). Optimize to 1D iterating w backwards. O(n×W).

column3: Solve unbounded knapsack or rod cutting.
column5: 1D DP: dp[w] = max(dp[w], dp[w−wt[i]] + val[i]). Iterate w forward. O(n×W).

column3: Solve coin change for minimum coins.
column5: dp[amt] = min(dp[amt], dp[amt − coin] + 1). O(amount × coins).

column3: Solve coin change for number of ways.
column5: dp[amt] += dp[amt − coin]. Outer loop on coins (not amounts) to avoid permutations. O(amount × coins).

column3: Solve partition equal subset sum.
column5: Target = sum/2. Boolean knapsack: dp[j] |= dp[j − nums[i]]. O(n × sum).

column3: Solve weighted job scheduling or maximum profit in job scheduling.
column5: Sort by end. Binary search last non-overlapping. dp[i] = max(dp[i−1], profit[i] + dp[j]). O(n log n).

column3: Count matrix paths with blocked cells.
column5: dp[i][j] = dp[i−1][j] + dp[i][j−1] if not blocked, else 0. O(m×n).

column3: Find the minimum path sum in a grid.
column5: dp[i][j] = grid[i][j] + min(dp[i−1][j], dp[i][j−1]). O(m×n).

column3: Find the minimum-cost path in a grid with constrained moves.
column5: DP if only down/right. Dijkstra if 4-directional with varying costs. O(m×n) or O(m×n log).

column3: Find the maximum path sum in a matrix.
column5: Top-down DP. dp[i][j] = val + max(up-left, up, up-right). O(m×n).

column3: Find the longest increasing path in a matrix.
column5: DFS + memoization from each cell. Try 4 directions where neighbor > current. O(m×n).

column3: Solve Cherry Pickup or its common 2-agent grid variants.
column5: 3D DP: two agents simultaneously. dp[r][c1][c2]. No double-counting same cell. O(m×n²).

column3: Solve Burst Balloons.
column5: dp[i][j] = max over k of dp[i][k] + dp[k][j] + nums[i]×nums[k]×nums[j]. O(n³).

column3: Solve matrix chain multiplication.
column5: dp[i][j] = min over k of dp[i][k] + dp[k+1][j] + cost(i,k,j). O(n³).

column3: Solve Boolean Parenthesization.
column5: Track true/false counts per subexpression. Combine with AND/OR/XOR operators. O(n³).

column3: Find the longest palindromic subsequence.
column5: LCS of string and its reverse. Or gap DP: dp[i][j] = dp[i+1][j−1]+2 if match. O(n²).

column3: Find the longest palindromic substring.
column5: Expand around center (odd + even). O(n²). Or Manacher's algorithm O(n).

column3: Solve Paint House or minimum-cost coloring variants.
column5: dp[i][color] = cost[i][color] + min(dp[i−1][other colors]). O(n×k).

column3: Partition an array into four non-empty parts minimizing gap of segment sums.
column5: Prefix sums + DP/binary search. Try all partition points, minimize max−min of segment sums.

column3: DP on trees or ancestor-jump queries with many queries.
column5: Binary lifting for LCA/kth ancestor. Or tree DP with rerooting technique. O(n log n) preprocessing.

column3: Given an equation string with one unknown X compute X.
column5: Parse equation, collect X coefficients on one side and constants on other. Solve linear equation.

column3: Implement substring search using KMP.
column5: Build failure/prefix function. Use it to skip re-comparing matched chars. O(n+m).

column3: Perform pattern search using the Z algorithm or rolling hash.
column5: Z-array: Z[i] = length of longest prefix match starting at i. Or Rabin-Karp rolling hash. O(n+m).

column3: Validate abbreviations or shorthand forms like i18n or A2S.
column5: Two pointers. Parse number from abbreviation, skip that many chars in word. Compare rest.

column3: Find the longest common prefix of a list of strings.
column5: Compare char by char across all strings. Or sort and compare first and last. O(n×m).

column3: Compress a string in place.
column5: Two pointers: read and write. Count consecutive chars, write char + count. O(n).

column3: Reverse a string in blocks or chunks of size k.
column5: Iterate in steps of 2k. Reverse first k chars in each block. O(n).

column3: Count subsequences that start with one character and end with another.
column5: Count start chars seen so far. When end char found, add count. O(n).

column3: Convert Roman numerals to integers.
column5: Map symbols to values. If current < next, subtract; else add. Left to right. O(n).

column3: Convert an integer to Roman numeral form.
column5: Greedy: map [1000,900,500,400,...,1] to symbols. Subtract largest, append symbol. O(1).

column3: Build a trie for insert search and prefix autocomplete.
column5: TrieNode: children map + isEnd. Insert/search/startsWith in O(L) per operation.

column3: Find the longest common prefix using a trie.
column5: Insert all strings. Walk root while node has single child and not end-of-word. O(total chars).

column3: Build a word dictionary that supports wildcard queries.
column5: Trie + DFS for '.' wildcard. On '.', try all children. O(26^dots × L) worst case.

column3: Design search suggestions or autocomplete using a trie.
column5: Walk trie to prefix node. DFS to collect top 3 lexicographic matches. O(n×L).

column3: Solve maximum XOR or XOR queries with a trie.
column5: Binary trie (MSB to LSB). For max XOR, greedily choose opposite bit. O(n × 32).

column3: Replace words in a sentence using dictionary roots stored in a trie.
column5: Build trie from roots. For each word, find shortest prefix match. Replace. O(n×L).

column3: Solve Word Search II using trie plus backtracking.
column5: Trie from words. Backtrack on grid from trie root. Prune when no match. O(m×n×4^L).

column3: Build a segment tree for range min max or sum queries.
column5: Build O(n). Query and update O(log n). Array-based or node-based.

column3: Use a segment tree with a custom range condition such as max − min < k.
column5: Store both min and max per node. Merge intervals. Query checks condition. O(n log n).

column3: Use a Fenwick tree or BIT for prefix sums and inversion-style queries.
column5: update(i, delta) and query(i) in O(log n). Great for prefix sums and inversions.

column3: Count inversions using a BIT or segment tree.
column5: Process right to left. Query BIT for count of smaller seen. Update. O(n log n).

column3: Support range updates and point queries with a BIT or segment tree.
column5: Difference array + BIT. Range update [l,r]: update(l, val), update(r+1, −val). Point query = prefix sum.

column3: Use binary lifting for kth ancestor or jump queries on a tree.
column5: Precompute up[v][k] = 2^k-th ancestor. Jump by decomposing k into powers of 2. O(n log n) build.

column3: Use a sparse table for static range minimum or maximum query.
column5: table[i][j] = min of 2^j elements from i. Query: overlap two blocks. O(n log n) build O(1) query.

column3: Design an LRU cache.
column5: HashMap + doubly linked list. Get/put O(1). Accessed → move to front. Evict from tail.

column3: Design a modified LRU cache where values are lists and get consumes from front.
column5: LRU + deque per key. Get pops front. If empty, remove entry.

column3: Design a string-keyed photo cache or similar cache with eviction policy.
column5: LRU/LFU with string keys. Track memory/count limits. HashMap + ordered structure.

column3: Design an LFU cache.
column5: HashMap + frequency buckets (doubly linked lists). Track minFreq. O(1) get/put.

column3: Design an All O(1) data structure supporting frequency updates.
column5: HashMap + doubly linked list of frequency buckets. inc/dec/getMax/getMin all O(1).

column3: Design a RandomizedSet with O(1) insert delete and get-random.
column5: HashMap (val→idx) + ArrayList. Delete: swap with last, pop. Random: random index. O(1).

column3: Design a RandomizedCollection that allows duplicates.
column5: HashMap (val→set of indices) + ArrayList. Swap-delete trick. Handle index sets. O(1) avg.

column3: Design a time-based key-value store.
column5: HashMap (key → list of (timestamp, value)). Binary search on timestamp for get. O(log n).

column3: Design browser history with back and forward navigation.
column5: Two stacks or doubly linked list. Visit clears forward. O(1) per operation.

column3: Design a circular deque.
column5: Array with front/rear pointers. Wrap with modulo. All operations O(1).

column3: Design a snapshot array.
column5: Per-index list of (snap_id, value). Binary search on snap_id for get. O(log S) get.

column3: Design an in-memory file system path structure.
column5: Trie of path components. Each node: children map, optional content, isFile flag.

column3: Design a leaderboard with score updates and top-k queries.
column5: HashMap + sorted structure (TreeMap). addScore/top/reset. O(log n) per op.

column3: Design a stock price fluctuation tracker.
column5: HashMap (timestamp→price) + TreeMap or two heaps for max/min. O(log n).

column3: Design a structure that supports add(x) and query(largest <= x).
column5: Balanced BST with floor operation. Or sorted list + binary search. O(log n) per op.

column3: Design an inverted index or word-search engine over a document collection.
column5: Map each word to document ID list. Multi-word = intersect posting lists. TF-IDF for ranking.

column3: Explain or implement external sorting for data larger than memory.
column5: Split into memory-sized chunks. Sort each. K-way merge with min-heap. O(n log n).

column3: Find the two numbers that appear once when every other appears twice.
column5: XOR all → xor of two uniques. Split by any set bit. XOR each group separately. O(n).

column3: Find the unique number when every other number appears three times.
column5: For each bit position, sum bits mod 3. Or use two bitmasks (ones, twos). O(n).

column3: Count set bits from 1 to n.
column5: For each bit position, count cycles of 0s and 1s. Or formula with highest set bit. O(log n).

column3: Check whether a number is a power of two or a power of four.
column5: Power of 2: n > 0 && (n & (n−1)) == 0. Power of 4: also no odd-position bits. O(1).

column3: Find a missing number and a repeated number using bit manipulation.
column5: XOR all elements and 1..n. Use set-bit to partition into two groups. O(n).

column3: Generate Gray code.
column5: i XOR (i >> 1) for i in 0..2ⁿ−1. Or recursive: reflect and prepend 0/1.

column3: Add two integers without using +.
column5: XOR for sum without carry. AND << 1 for carry. Repeat until carry = 0. O(32).

column3: Divide two integers without using / * or %.
column5: Subtract divisor shifted left (powers of 2). Binary long division. O(32).

column3: Compute Euler's totient function.
column5: phi(n) = n × ∏(1 − 1/p) for prime factors p. Factorize first. O(√n).

column3: Count trailing zeroes in a factorial.
column5: Count factors of 5: n/5 + n/25 + n/125 + ... O(log n).

column3: Compute fast power x^n in O(log n).
column5: Binary exponentiation. Odd → x × power(x, n−1). Even → power(x², n/2). Handle negative n.

column3: Convert a number from one base to another.
column5: To base 10 first (multiply by base powers). Then to target (repeated division). O(digits).

column3: Convert a fraction to recurring decimal form.
column5: Long division. HashMap of remainder → position. When remainder repeats, insert parentheses.

column3: Use gcd or lcm reasoning for problems like the water jug puzzle.
column5: Euclidean gcd. lcm = a×b/gcd. Water jug: solvable iff target % gcd(a,b) == 0.

column3: Generate primes in a range using sieve-based techniques.
column5: Sieve of Eratosthenes: mark multiples. O(n log log n). Segmented sieve for large ranges.

column3: Compute modular exponentiation and reason about modular inverse basics.
column5: Binary exp with mod. Inverse: power(a, mod−2, mod) when mod is prime (Fermat's little theorem).

column3: Check whether a number is a perfect square without directly using square root.
column5: Binary search on [0, n]. Check mid×mid == n. Or Newton's method. O(log n).

column3: Solve the bulb-switching problem.
column5: Bulb stays on iff toggled odd times = perfect square index. Answer = floor(√n).

column3: Count cubes with 0 1 2 or 3 painted faces after painting outside.
column5: 3 faces: 8 corners. 2 faces: 12(n−2) edges. 1 face: 6(n−2)². 0 faces: (n−2)³.

column3: Handle square-root exponentiation overflow and modular-arithmetic edge cases.
column5: Use long/BigInteger. Check overflow before multiply. Apply mod consistently.

column3: Find the length of the longest run of consecutive 1s in a binary array.
column5: Linear scan. Reset counter on 0, update max on 1. O(n).
